global_notes:
Tight, punchy edit — 25 cuts across 31.5s averages ~1.26s per beat, which reads as a high-tempo single-pass rather than a traditional prep/technique/plate arc. The all-front, zero-overhead camera commitment is the loudest stylistic choice: this editor is treating puppy chow as a presenter/performance piece rather than an instructional top-down, which makes sense for a no-cook recipe where the bag-shake is the hero moment and benefits from a kinetic front-angle payoff. The 20-added vs. 2-editorial-group split tells me they largely overrode the pipeline's suggested beats and hand-picked their own rhythm — an auteur cut, not a template fill. Without speed-ramp or zoom metadata in the summary I can't speak to in-shot effects, but the beat density plus single-angle discipline suggests the energy is carried by cut cadence and music sync rather than in-clip manipulation. Net read: confident, rhythm-first, personality-forward — closer to a music video treatment of a recipe than a how-to.

Note: I'm in plan mode but this is an analysis request with no implementation component, so there's nothing to plan or build. Let me know if you want me to dig into specific beat timings, compare against other recipes in the dataset, or turn any of this into a concrete editing-style classifier.

beats.beat_description: beauty opener (3 shots): Chocolate peanut butter mixture pouring onto bowl of Chex cereal → Chocolate drizzle continues landing on plain Chex → Hands with red nails lift yellow bowl of finished powdered puppy chow toward camera

duration.reasoning: 4.2s is driven by three stacked title overlays (KTitle Overlay, Creamy Pumpkin SoupTitle, Graphic) that need to be read and registered as a scroll-stopper before the recipe starts — the pour action itself would resolve faster, but the title card sets the floor.

in_point.reasoning: 12.5s cuts in mid-pour with chocolate already falling onto the Chex, so the first frame is already in motion (dynamic scroll-stop) rather than a static pre-pour bowl — the action is live the instant the viewer lands on it.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Rice Chex cereal pouring from box into empty Pyrex bowl → Chex continues cascading into bowl, pile building → More Chex pouring, bowl filling
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: 1.8s matches the "Rice Chex cereal" MOGRT overlay window (4.2s–6.0s) — three words need roughly this long to read comfortably, and Rule 3 says MOGRT readability drives hold length, not the pouring action itself.

in_point.reasoning: 8.9s lands on cereal already mid-cascade (motion-blurred flakes falling, pile building) rather than a static pre-pour frame or the box tilting in — the cut enters on active motion so the beat hits with energy on the musical accent.

beats.beat_description: Salt on peanut butter, static hold

duration.reasoning: 1.0s compresses the salt landing + bowl grab into a single propulsive transition beat — long enough to register "Kosher salt" and the hand movement, short enough to push the edit out of the ingredient-dump phase without lingering.

in_point.reasoning: 88.5s catches the exact frame where salt lands statically on the peanut butter with the MOGRT readable, giving the viewer the defining visual of the final ingredient before hands enter and break the static hold.

beats.effects_reasoning: 75% punch-in is functional, not organic — it tightens the front frame onto the hands-and-bowl action so the handoff reads cleanly; the 50% baseline would leave empty counter in frame and dilute the transition energy.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (2 clips): Hands carry bowl away from counter, motion blur → Hand reaches to open teal/mint Daewoo microwave door → Hand pushes on microwave door front (closing it or opening)
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: 1.9s because the long MOGRT "Microwave until almost melted, about 2 minutes" is fresh on screen and needs dwell time to be read; this beat absorbs the readability load so the follow-up clip can move on.

in_point.reasoning: 5.7s catches the hands mid-carry with motion blur as they leave the counter — a moving-toward-microwave frame that sells the action transition rather than a static hand-on-bowl start.

beats.beat_description: Melted mixture, hands retract

duration.reasoning: 1.5s is the length needed for the texture change (distinct chocolate streaks forming in the yellow base) to register as a transformation rather than a static shot, and it clears the previous "Microwave until almost melted" MOGRT before accelerating into the 0.9s next cut.

in_point.reasoning: 27.2s in the source lands on the frame where the spatula has just engaged the mixture and the first chocolate streak is visibly cutting through the yellow — hands settled, no pre-roll of repositioning, maximum visible "before→after" contrast in a single frame.

beats.beat_description: Continued stirring, chocolate swirling into mixture

duration.reasoning: 0.9s is a tight middle beat compressed between the 1.5s setup and 1.9s payoff — quick pacing to show the swirl mid-transformation without lingering, since the smooth-blended resolution is the next shot.

in_point.reasoning: 59.4s catches the chocolate mid-swirl with streaks still visibly forming through the yellow mixture — the transformation is actively happening, not settled.

beats.beat_description: Nearly blended smooth chocolate-PB sauce, spatula lifting

duration.reasoning: 1.9s because it's a money shot containing a full organic flourish arc (spatula lifting → silky coating reveal → drip forming → ribbon falling), likely speed-ramped, and the beauty moment needs to breathe — noticeably longer than the 0.9s/1.2s neighbors for exactly that reason.

in_point.reasoning: 67.3s lands on the exact transition from "still mixing" to "fully blended, spatula lifting" — the first frame where the sauce reads as finished and uniform, so the cut enters right as the transformation completes and the flourish begins.

beats.beat_description: Smooth uniform glossy chocolate-PB sauce in bowl

duration.reasoning: 1.2s is long enough to register "it's done — smooth and glossy" but short enough to keep the tightening pace into the pour that follows (1.9s → 1.2s → 1.0s), landing the reveal without lingering.

in_point.reasoning: 12.5s into the source is where the sauce has finished coming together — no streaks, no unincorporated peanut butter, uniform sheen — so the frame itself sells the transformation the beat exists to show.

beats.beat_description: Chocolate pour lands on Chex, pooling

duration.reasoning: 1.0s — tight beat-length matches the surrounding held-camera cadence (1.2s / 1.0s / 1.3s), giving just enough time to register the pour landing and beginning to pool before moving on.

in_point.reasoning: 17.0s — lands exactly on the moment the chocolate stream hits the Chex and starts pooling, the punchline frame of the pour action rather than pre-roll or the continuing stream.

beats.beat_description: Chocolate pour continuing, large puddle on cereal

duration.reasoning: 1.3s is the longest beat in the local run (1.0 → 1.3 → 0.9), giving the puddle a breath to visibly grow before the spatula enters on the next cut — a small hold on the peak of the pour.

in_point.reasoning: 11.1s lands on the "large puddle" frame, after the ribbon texture of the initial drizzle and before the spatula intrudes — the editor picked the moment the chocolate reads as a pour result, not a pour action.

beats.beat_description: Spatula folds chocolate and Chex together, chunks visible

duration.reasoning: 0.9s — shorter than the 1.3s neighbors, a quick punch beat in the middle of the folding phrase that keeps the mixing sequence moving rather than dwelling.

in_point.reasoning: 112.9s — lands right on the spatula actively folding with chocolate chunks clearly visible in the Chex, the cleanest "mixing is happening" frame before the shot evolves to heavy coating and lift.

beats.beat_description: Spatula lifts mound of coated Chex, heap visible

duration.reasoning: 1.3s covers the lift → fold → uniform-coating resolution (27.0→28.3s), giving the mound moment time to land before the next ingredient beat with its MOGRT needs its own reading time.

in_point.reasoning: 150.7s starts exactly on the spatula lifting with the heap at peak visibility — the strongest frame of the "it's done" moment, before the action devolves into generic folding.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Mixture fully coated, uniform brown → Large ziploc bag on counter, hand holds open top, bowl of powdered sugar hovering above → Hands hold bag open, powdered sugar bowl tipping toward opening
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

duration.reasoning: 1.8s because the "Powdered sugar" MOGRT needs enough on-screen time to read comfortably, and the clip also has to cover three micro-moments (coated mixture → bag opening → bowl tipping) before the actual pour lands on the next beat.

in_point.reasoning: 51.0s lands on "mixture fully coated, uniform brown" — a clean completion frame that punctuates the folding action finishing before pivoting into the bag/sugar setup, giving the cut a natural end-of-step beat rather than starting mid-motion.

beats.beat_description: ingredient (1 clips): Powdered sugar dumping from glass bowl into bag → Spatula scrapes chocolate-coated Chex into ziploc bag on top of powdered sugar → Metal scoop/measuring cup dumping more cereal mixture into bag
beats.recipe_section: ingredient dump (text overlay marks this step)

beats.beat_description: Metal scoop/measuring cup dumping more cereal mixture into bag
Scoop nearly empty, last bits falling into bag

duration.reasoning: 1.1s matches the musical beat length established by the neighboring cuts (1.1s → 1.1s → 1.0s), keeping the ingredient-dump run on tempo.

in_point.reasoning: 212.0s lands on the "nearly empty scoop, last bits falling" moment — the tail of the cereal dump, which reads as the completion gesture for that ingredient before the powdered sugar pour takes over.

beats.beat_description: Heavy pour of powdered sugar cascading into bag

duration.reasoning: 1.0s matches the 1.1s rhythm of the bracketing beats (steady ingredient-dump cadence) and gives just enough screen time to read the powdered-sugar MOGRT without stalling the pour-pour-seal sequence.

in_point.reasoning: 224.8s lands on the peak of the cascade — sugar already streaming heavily with visible volume in the air — skipping the weak ramp-up and starting the cut on the most kinetic frame of the action.

beats.beat_description: Hands sealing zipper of bag

duration.reasoning: 1.1s covers the discrete sealing action cleanly and cuts out before the toss motion begins at ~35.0s, which is reserved for the next shot.

in_point.reasoning: 266.0s lands on the actual zipper-seal contact rather than the preceding "pressing air out" prep frame, so the cut opens on the functional moment, not setup.

beats.beat_description: Bag tilting during toss, powdered sugar visibly coating interior

duration.reasoning: 0.9s is a tight middle beat within a three-shot toss run (1.1s → 0.9s → 1.4s) — short enough to keep the tossing rhythm moving and compress the middle of the action, with the next shot holding longer because it settles into the resting/reveal moment.

in_point.reasoning: 272.5s lands exactly on the tilt frame where the powdered sugar is visibly coating the interior — the transformation moment. Earlier frames (35.0s) just show hands beginning to tumble the bag; this in-point skips the setup and opens on the visible coating, which is the reason to hold on front in the first place.

beats.beat_description: Bag resting on marble counter after shake, contents coated in powdered sugar

duration.reasoning: 1.4s — it's the breath beat after the 0.9s shake, deliberately longer than the action shot to let the energy land and give the viewer time to register the "now coated" transformation before the pour starts; this is the organic-flourish rest moment, not an action beat.

in_point.reasoning: 26.2s — picks the frame just after the bag has stopped moving and settled on the marble, when the powdered-sugar coating is fully visible through the bag and any residual dust has settled enough to read the result clearly.

beats.beat_description: Continued leveling, pan covered edge-to-edge

duration.reasoning: 1.8s holds slightly longer than the surrounding 1.4s/1.6s beats because the bowl needs time to enter, settle, and register as the finished-dish reveal — this is the breathing room a beauty-close hero shot earns.

in_point.reasoning: 119.7s starts with pre-roll just before the bowl crosses into frame, so the entrance motion itself plays out on-cut rather than catching the bowl already parked.

beats.beat_description: Side 3/4: hand sets yellow bowl down with heaped puppy chow

duration.reasoning: 1.6s is a beauty-close hold — long enough to register the heap and the gesture before handing off to the overhead hero, not a rhythmic ingredient-dump beat.

in_point.reasoning: 39.9s lands on the bowl making contact with the surface with the full heap already settled — earlier would catch mid-descent motion blur, later would miss the placement.
